relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus enters the synagogue where a withered hand waits.
The watchers turn healing into a trap.
Jesus brings the wounded hand into the center.
He asks whether Sabbath serves good, life, and restoration.
Anger and grief meet hardened hearts, and the hand is restored.
Mercy provokes opposition between unlikely powers.
Jesus withdraws to the sea, and multitudes follow.
People come from every direction because of what he is doing.
The crowd presses so intensely that a boat must be ready.
Need reaches toward touch.
Unclean spirits recognize him as Son of the Divine.
He refuses premature disclosure from distorted sources.
On the mountain he calls those he wants.
The Twelve are appointed to be with him and be sent.
Authority is given for release from unclean forces.
Simon is named Peter.
James and John receive the thunder-name.
The circle of the Twelve continues.
Judas is named with the shadow of handing-over.
The house fills until even eating is impossible.
Those near him misunderstand the intensity of his mission.
Scribes explain Spirit-release through accusation.
Jesus answers with the logic that an opposing power cannot undo itself.
A divided reign cannot stand.
A divided house cannot stand.
An opposing field divided against itself collapses.
A strong house is emptied only when its grip is first restrained.
Release is wide for humanity’s misalignments and blasphemies.
But calling holy Spirit’s work unclean closes the channel of release.
The warning is aimed at their mislabeling of Spirit-work.
His mother and brothers stand outside calling him.
The crowd tells him his family seeks him.
Jesus asks what true family is.
He looks around the circle and names a new kinship.
Doing the Divine will makes brother, sister, and mother.
